headline: Quiet, unconfirmed news flow for Turkey as sticky ~32% inflation and the CBRT easing path remain the dominant macro backdrop.
summary: I could not verify any specific market-moving Turkey finance story from the last 24 hours in the available search results, so this briefing reflects the most recent verifiable data points rather than confirmed same-day developments. The key backdrop remains disinflation that has stalled and partly reversed, with annual CPI last reported around 32.6% (May data), against a central bank that had been gradually cutting its policy rate into 2026. Readers should treat the items below as the latest confirmable context, not as breaking July 1 headlines, and verify current levels directly at the linked sources.

items.title: Turkish annual inflation last reported at 32.61% (May 2026), edging up from 32.37% in April
items.summary: The most recent official CPI reading showed annual inflation at 32.61% in May, up from 32.37% in April and reversing earlier disinflation, partly reflecting higher energy costs; June data was not yet available. This keeps pressure on the central bank's easing plans.

items.title: CBRT cut its policy rate to 38% in December 2025 amid 'improving' signals
items.summary: The Central Bank of the Republic of Türkiye lowered its benchmark rate to 38% in December 2025, continuing a gradual easing cycle; the direction of subsequent 2026 meetings is central to lira and bond pricing. This is background context, not a fresh decision.

items.title: CBRT reaffirmed its 5% medium-term inflation target and published a 2026 policy calendar
items.summary: The central bank set out its 2026 monetary policy meeting calendar and restated its 5% inflation objective, underscoring its commitment to orthodox disinflation. It frames market expectations for the pace of rate cuts through 2026.
